preamble: The transformation of the high front rounded vowel [y] (or its related labialized glides) into the voiceless bilabial stop [p] is a rare form of fortition and labialization, documented in specific language developments where vowels harden into consonants.

examples.note: Regular shift where historical rounded vowels or labialized glides fortified into bilabial stops (e.g., Bats 'eb lar' corresponding to Chechen stems).
